old_claim.note: Public Notice Boards is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim_absent: No direct public-notice-board dependency remains because the node is scoped to theater venue access, tickets, subsidies, seating, finance, and public records rather than generic notice-posting infrastructure.

source_shape.source_locator: The review identifies fourth-century theatre buildings, massive audiences, organization, funding of performances, and finance and records in Athens, but not public notice boards as a ticket-sales prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports theater infrastructure and finance/records, not generic public notice-board infrastructure.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping public_notice_boards direct would turn a possible administrative communication channel into a prerequisite for ticket access and seating finance.

ontology_before: Theater Ticket Sales depended on Public Notice Boards, implying notice-board infrastructure was part of the ticketing/access mechanism.
ontology_after: Theater Ticket Sales depends on public theater infrastructure and currency; notice boards remain broader civic communications context outside the direct prerequisite set.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: public_notice_boards is no longer a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to posted theater schedules or public notices rather than ticket access.
A stronger source showed Athenian theater ticket distribution required notice-board infrastructure.
A separate theater_public_notices node replaced the ticket-sales scope.
short_reason: Notice boards are communications context, not theater ticketing infrastructure.
